data.title: Invitation Homes upgraded, AMH downgraded at Citi
data.description:
Citi upgraded Invitation Homes (NYSE:INVH) to Buy from Neutral and downgraded AMH (NYSE:AMH), formerly American Homes 4 Rent, to Neutral from Buy as analyst Eric Wolfe expects INVH's earnings will catch up to AMH's within the next year.

He also pointed out that Invitation Homes' (NYSE:INVH) stock is trading an ~30 basis point discount to AMH (NYSE:AMH) on an implied cap-rate basis after its recent underperformance since Q1 earnings. That's at the lower end of its historical range.

"We believe INVH’s YoY core FFO growth will be +5% by the end of 2025 and generally in-line with AMH next year in the 5-6% range," Wolfe wrote in a note to clients.

In addition, Invitation's (INVH) lending program is likely to lead to additional acquisition opportunities, he said. "While there’s certainly incremental risk to this program given that INVH is funding horizontal and vertical development (at ~75% LTVs & 3-5 year terms), we believe INVH is in a good position to assess these risks given its knowledge of BTR (build-to-rent) rents and valuations in the market," Wolfe wrote.

Invitation Homes (INVH) stock gained 1.3%, and AMH (AMH) stock increased 0.5% in Friday morning trading.

Citi's Buy rating on Invitation (INVH) contrast with the SA Quant rating of Hold and aligns with the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

The Neutral rating on AMH (AMH) agrees with the SA Quant rating of Hold and diverges from the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

data.title: Trump again pushes Fed to cut rates after ECB's slash
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump continued to urge the Federal Reserve to lower interest rates, this time after the ECB slashed its monetary policy.

"'Too Late' at the Fed is a disaster! Europe has had 10 rate cuts, we have had none. Despite him, our Country is doing great. Go for a full point, Rocket Fuel!," Trump said in a post on Truth Social. 

On Wednesday, the President had asked the Fed to lower interest rates after private hiring in May reached its lowest level in over two years

The European Central Bank (ECB) slashed its interest rates by 25 basis points to 2.00% on Thursday as inflation in the eurozone slipped below target.

Trump's latest criticism of the Fed comes about a week after he met the central bank chief Jerome Powell "to discuss economic developments, including growth, employment, and inflation."

Last month, Trump had called Powell a "fool" a day after the central bank held its key interest rate steady, as widely expected. Powell's term as chair expires in May 202

The Fed reduced its federal funds rate target range by 100 basis points starting in December but has held the rates at its current range of 4.25%-4.50% since the beginning of 2025.

Wall Street's major averages jumped on Friday, and yields rose, as the May nonfarm payrolls beat consensus. 

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Stablecoins' expanding presence could stir volatility in short-term Treasuries - report
data.description:
Stablecoins are gaining traction as a mainstream financial instrument, though their rise could introduce new sources of volatility to short-dated U.S. Treasury securities, according to a media report published Friday.

Indeed, such dollar-pegged cryptocurrencies already have a large presence in the U.S. Treasury market, with USD Coin (USDC-USD), tether (USDT-USD) and the likes holding a large portion of their reserves in Treasury bills, which are highly regarded as the equivalent of cash.

The combined market cap of the USDC and USDT totaled a little over $115B at press time, according to data from CoinMarketCap. The total stablecoin market value stood at about $253B.

While some argue that proposed stablecoin legislation will drive further growth in stablecoin activity, others caution that as stablecoins expand their presence, the connection to a young, more volatile industry could bring additional volatility to short-term government debt.

"A problem in the stablecoin sector could spill over into broader financial markets, affecting institutions holding similar assets or (that) rely on stablecoin liquidity," said Cristiano Ventricelli, vice president and senior analyst of digital assets at Moody’s Ratings, Reuters reported.

"In the event of a sudden loss of confidence, regulatory pressure, or market rumors, this could trigger large-scale liquidations, potentially depressing Treasury prices and disrupting fixed-income markets," he added.

Meanwhile, as soon as next week, Congress may pass a key stablecoin bill that would create comprehensive regulatory guidelines for the tokens, the article said, including rules for requiring stablecoin issuers to disclose monthly audited reports regarding their reserve mix.

The bill would also require stablecoins to always be backed by liquid, safe assets, such as the U.S. dollar and T-bills. This would effectively make stablecoins a permanent marginal buyer of short-term government debt.

Circle Internet Group (CRCL), the issuer of USD Coin (USDC-USD), debuted on the New York Stock Exchange on Thursday following a strong initial public offering. The stock scored triple-digit gains in its first day of trading.

data.title: Pfizer, Arvinas submit marketing application for vepdegestrant to the FDA
data.description:
• Arvinas (NASDAQ:ARVN) said on Friday that it has filed for a marketing application with the U.S. FDA, along with its partner Pfizer (NYSE:PFE), for vepdegestrant in advanced breast cancer treatment.
  • This submission is based on promising results from a late-stage trial evaluating vepdegestrant versus fulvestrant.
  • Vepdegestrant has received Fast Track status from the U.S. FDA for treating adults with ER+/HER2- advanced or metastatic breast cancer after endocrine therapy.

data.title: S&P pops over 6,000, Nasdaq and Dow also rally as labor report beats consensus
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages jumped on Friday, and yields rose, as the labor report showed slower growth in May but still reported a healthy gain.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) is +1.2% as the index marched back above 6,000 for the first time since February 26.

Also climbing is the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) which is +1.3%, and the Dow (DJI) which is +1.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 8 basis points to 4.47%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 7 basis points to 4.01%.

May Nonfarm payrolls: +139K vs. +126K consensus and +147K prior (revised from +177K), according to data released by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics on Friday.

Unemployment rate: 4.2% vs. 4.2% consensus and 4.2% prior. 

The indication of a potential thaw in tensions between Musk and Trump comes after a public feud that erupted between them on Thursday. Earlier, their clash escalated when the president threatened to terminate Musk’s government contracts, leading to a 14% drop in Tesla’s (TSLA) shares.

Wall Street closed in the red as Tesla (TSLA) shaved off more than $100B in market capitalization amid Trump and Musk's verbal spat. 

"Markets had a volatile session yesterday, as they grappled with a barrage of news that each pushed in different directions. Those included positive U.S.-China headlines amid a call between Trump and Xi, a hawkish ECB decision, and more weak data from the U.S. But the most remarkable was an extraordinary war of words between Trump and Elon Musk that ultimately left risk assets losing ground," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

The trade headlines outweighed an initial negative reaction to the latest weekly U.S. jobless claims, which added to fears that the U.S. labor market was finally deteriorating after Liberation Day, Reid added. 

Trump on Thursday said that he had held a "very good phone call" with China's leader Xi Jinping which "resulted in a very positive conclusion" for the two nations.

data.title: Arcutis says Zoryve data demonstrates durable atopic dermatitis improvements
data.description:
• New phase 3 data released by Arcutis Biotherapeutics (NASDAQ:ARQT) on Zoryve (roflumilast) found that the cream leads to durable improvement in of atopic dermatitis symptoms over the long term.
  • Results from the open-label INTEGUMENT-OLE study found that in children 2-5 years old who saw disease clearance and then switched to proactive twice-weekly application Zoryve, the median duration of disease control was 238 days.
  • For adults down to children age 6, the figure was 281 days.
  • The new data will be presented at the 2025 Revolutionizing Atopic Dermatitis Conference.

data.title: SolarBank renews at-the-market equity program
data.description:
• SolarBank (NASDAQ:SUUN) Friday announced that following the filing of its new short form base shelf prospectus, it is proceeding with a renewal of its at-the-market equity program.
  • The company is required to renew the ATM Program due to the expiry of the company's 2023 short form base shelf prospectus.
  • Under the distribution agreement, the company may issue common shares of the company having an aggregate offering price of up to $15,000,000 under ‎the ATM program.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: CRCL, CAG, MANU and NVO
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Friday surged after a strong May jobs report. Here are some stocks to watch on Friday: 

  • Shares of Circle Internet (NYSE:CRCL) were +15.3% in pre-market trading, a day after the stock saw a blockbuster debut on the New York Stock Exchange. The stablecoin issuer raised nearly $1.1B in an upsized initial public offering (IPO). Its shares opened at $69 on Thursday, more than double the IPO price of $31 per share and valuing the stock at nearly $18B. Circle's (NYSE:CRCL) debut is a shot in the arm for an IPO market that has been struggling.
  • Conagra Brands (NYSE:CAG) stock was +0.3% ahead of the opening bell. The consumer foods company said it would sell its Van de Kamp's and Mrs. Paul's frozen seafood brands for $55M in cash to High Liner Foods. The products from those brands that were a part of the deal contributed about $75M to Conagra's (NYSE:CAG) 2024 net sales. "This divestiture allows us to further focus our efforts on strengthening our core frozen offerings," CEO Sean Connolly said.
  • Class A shares of Manchester United (NYSE:MANU) were +5.4% ahead of market open, after the famed soccer club raised its fiscal full year 2025 adjusted EBITDA guidance to £180M-£190M from a prior range of £145M-£160M. The company's men's team had a strong Europa League, reaching the finals where they lost to Tottenham. However, the team had its worst Premier League season since they were relegated in 1974, finishing in 15th position. 
  • U.S.-listed shares of Novo Nordisk (NVO) were in focus, +1.9% in pre-market trading. Europe's drug regulator on Friday said that its safety committee had concluded that Novo’s (NVO) blockbuster weight loss/diabetes therapy semaglutide could cause a side effect in the eyes that could lead to vision loss in very rare cases. The committee recommended that semaglutide's product information be revised to reflect its potential to cause the condition.
